world.title: ‘India should now view Pakistan’s success on Iran as a zero sum game’
world.summary: India should consider Pakistan's diplomatic achievements with Iran as a zero-sum game, according to a recent analysis. The article discusses the implications of Pakistan's growing influence in the region and suggests that India must reassess its strategies in response. The newsletter aims to provide insights into Indian politics and the evolving dynamics between India, Pakistan, and Iran.

world.title: 'You touched me without my permission’: Internet outraged as old Pakistan interview resurfaces showing man touching reporter mid
world.summary: A resurfaced street interview video from Pakistan is going viral, prompting significant online outrage. The clip features a man touching a reporter without her consent, leading to strong reactions from social media users. Although the video is not new, its re-emergence has sparked renewed discussions about consent and appropriate behavior in public interactions, highlighting ongoing societal issues.

world.title: Shattered windows, buildings, cars on fire: Russia rains 31 missiles, 636 drones on Ukraine, killing 14
world.summary: Russian missile and drone strikes targeted Ukraine overnight, resulting in the deaths of at least 14 individuals, including a child, according to local authorities. The assault involved 31 missiles and 636 drones, causing significant damage to buildings and vehicles. This incident marks one of the latest escalations in the ongoing conflict between Russia and Ukraine.

business.title: Maruti Suzuki loses grip on India’s car market, share sinks to a 13-year low in FY26
business.summary: Maruti Suzuki's market share in India's passenger vehicle sector fell to 39.26 percent in FY26, the lowest in 13 years. This decline of nearly 12 percentage points since FY20 reflects increased competition and changing consumer preferences. Once holding about half of the market, the company now faces challenges in maintaining its position in the world's third-largest car market.

business.title: CAM Partners Anchal Dhir and Shubham Rastogi resign to join Anagram Partners
business.summary: Anchal Dhir and Shubham Rastogi have resigned from CAM Partners to join Anagram Partners. Both lawyers had been integral to CAM's Corporate practice since the start of their careers. CAM Managing Partner Cyril Shroff acknowledged their contributions, particularly praising Dhir's legal skills and character, expressing his continued support and wishing them well in their future endeavors.

business.title: Who was Mark Mobius and why was the $40 billion India bull famous as ‘Indiana Jones of emerging markets’
business.summary: Mark Mobius, a prominent investor, passed away at 89. Known as the "Indiana Jones of emerging markets," he championed investment in India, viewing opportunities amid perceived risks. Mobius traveled extensively, engaging with local businesses and leaders. His insights attracted billions to markets deemed volatile, emphasizing the importance of understanding markets through their people and fostering confidence in emerging economies.

technology.title: Motorola Edge 70 Pro Camera, Battery, Display, Chipset, Colours - All Specs Confirmed Ahead Of April 22 Launch
technology.summary: Motorola has confirmed the launch of the Edge 70 Pro in India on April 22. The device features a 6.8-inch AMOLED display with 1.5K resolution, continuing the brand's curved display tradition. This announcement follows the recent release of the Edge 70 Fusion in March. A dedicated microsite is now available on Flipkart for further details.

technology.title: Apple and Google host ‘nudify’ apps with 483 million downloads despite policy bans: Report
technology.summary: Apple and Google are under scrutiny for hosting "nudify" apps that facilitate the creation of non-consensual sexual images, despite their policies against such content. A report from the Tech Transparency Project highlights that these apps have collectively amassed 483 million downloads. The findings raise concerns about the companies' enforcement of their own guidelines amid increasing global pressure for stricter regulations.

technology.title: iPhone 16e price drops below Rs 43,000, should you buy it?
technology.summary: The iPhone 16e is currently priced below Rs 43,000 on Zepto, discounted from its launch price of Rs 59,900. The listed price is Rs 44,327, with additional bank offers available, potentially reducing the effective cost by up to Rs 2,200. This promotion raises questions about whether now is a good time to purchase the device.

sports.title: Man Charged with In-Flight Sexual Assault in Perth
sports.summary: An Indian national has been charged with allegedly sexually assaulting a female passenger during an international flight to Perth. The incident reportedly occurred while the man was seated next to the victim. He is expected to appear in Perth Magistrates Court to face the charges. Further details regarding the case have not been disclosed at this time.

sports.title: Experienced Bangladesh face inexperienced NZ in hot Dhaka
sports.summary: Bangladesh is set to face New Zealand in Dhaka, with the latter missing several first-choice players due to commitments in the IPL and PSL. This matchup presents a significant opportunity for Bangladesh to capitalize on New Zealand's inexperience. The match is anticipated to be played in hot conditions, potentially impacting player performance and strategy.

sports.title: 'Saari nazar utaar denge': PSL CEO wards off 'evil eye' for Pakistan's Babar Azam, video goes viral - Watch
sports.summary: A light-hearted moment featuring PSL CEO Salman Naseer and captain Babar Azam gained attention after Peshawar Zalmi's victory over Quetta Gladiators. In a viral video, Naseer is seen performing a gesture to ward off the 'evil eye' for Azam. The incident highlights the camaraderie and spirit within the team following their recent success in the tournament.

health.title: Latest fertility treatment advancements in India: Genetic testing in IVF - Boon or ethical concern?
health.summary: Dr. Hrishikesh Pai discusses advancements in fertility treatments in India, particularly the role of genetic testing in IVF. He highlights its clinical applications and the ethical concerns surrounding its use. The growth in fertility treatments is attributed to changing lifestyles, delayed marriages, and increased awareness, leading many couples to seek assistance in conceiving a child.

health.title: Genetic testing in India: Debunking myths, understanding risks, and how DNA insights are transforming preventive healthcare
health.summary: Genetic testing in India is increasingly popular, despite ongoing myths. It offers significant benefits for preventive healthcare by providing insights that can guide medical decisions. However, there are also risks associated with genetic testing that need to be understood. As awareness grows, the role of DNA insights in shaping healthcare strategies continues to evolve, impacting patient outcomes positively.

health.title: Are microplastics in ovaries impacting reproductive health?
health.summary: Research indicates that microplastics may negatively affect ovarian health and reproductive outcomes. The presence of these particles in the body raises concerns about their impact on gynaecological health. Experts emphasize the need for increased awareness and preventive measures to address potential risks associated with microplastics, highlighting the importance of understanding their effects on human reproductive systems.

science.title: The incredible story of the vacuum from 'horror vacui' to quantum physics
science.summary: The concept of vacuum has evolved in physics, challenging the notion of emptiness. In everyday terms, a vacuum may seem devoid of matter, but advanced physics reveals complexities. Quantum physics, for instance, suggests that vacuums can contain fluctuating energy and virtual particles. This shift in understanding illustrates how perceptions of emptiness vary with scientific advancements and theoretical frameworks.

science.title: Exploring the Invisible: The Vacuum's Compelling Secrets
science.summary: The vacuum, once thought to be empty, reveals complex physics. This exploration traces the evolution from Aristotle's horror vacui to significant discoveries like Torricelli's void. It highlights the transition to modern quantum field theory, demonstrating that vacuums possess measurable properties. The article emphasizes the intricate nature of vacuums, challenging traditional notions of emptiness in physics.

science.title: Triassic reptile discovery reveals ancient crocodile cousin that may have shifted to two
science.summary: A newly studied Triassic reptile, Sonselasuchus cedrus, is challenging existing beliefs about the locomotion of ancient crocodile relatives. This small creature, part of the shuvosaurid group, may have transitioned from a four-legged to a two-legged stance during its life. This finding suggests a significant developmental change in movement among early reptiles, providing new insights into their evolution.

entertainment.title: Netflix 'Toaster' Movie Review: Rajkummar Rao and Sanya Malhotra’s dark comedy is a deeply engaging watch
entertainment.summary: Netflix's "Toaster," directed by Vivek Daschaudary, features Rajkummar Rao and Sanya Malhotra in a dark comedy exploring themes of stinginess. The film, presented in Hindi, includes a supporting cast of Archana Puran Singh, Abhishek Banerjee, and others. While the narrative is engaging, critiques note that the script loses momentum towards the conclusion, impacting overall coherence.

entertainment.title: Madras High Court dismisses actress Tamannaah Bhatia's ₹1 crore claim against Power Soaps
entertainment.summary: The Madras High Court has dismissed actress Tamannaah Bhatia's ₹1 crore claim against Power Soaps. The dispute originated from a 2008 agreement allowing the company to use her images for promotional purposes until October 2009. Bhatia contended that Power Soaps continued to use her images after the agreement expired, leading to her legal claim.

entertainment.title: Dhurandhar mints ₹3000 crore: How Aditya Dhar built India's biggest franchise, beat YRF Spy Universe with just 2 films
entertainment.summary: Aditya Dhar's two films in the Dhurandhar franchise have collectively grossed over ₹3000 crore at the box office. This achievement positions Dhurandhar to surpass the total earnings of the YRF Spy Universe. The success highlights Dhar's impact on the Indian film industry, establishing him as a significant figure with only two releases to his credit.
